perturbation.original_text: 1. You hereby agree that the Confidential Information will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and implementing the Transaction and that such information will be kept confidential by you; provided that any such information may be disclosed to your officers, directors, employees, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, consultants, other agents or representatives and financing sources (such Persons hereinafter collectively being referred to as your “ Representatives ” who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and implementing the Transaction, have been informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information, and have been advised that such information is to be kept confidential, and provided , further , that such officers, directors, employees, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, consultants, other agents or representatives and financing sources shall not be deemed to be your Representatives unless (but solely to the extent that) you furnish Confidential Information to them. You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives. You and the Company also each agree, on behalf of itself, its affiliates and its and their respective Representatives, that it shall not, without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, disclose to any Person the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to you, that discussions or negotiations have taken place or are taking place concerning a possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. You hereby agree that the Confidential Information will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and implementing the Transaction and that such information will be kept confidential by you; provided that any such information may be disclosed to your officers, directors, employees, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, consultants, other agents or representatives and financing sources who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and implementing the Transaction. You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives. You and the Company also each agree, on behalf of itself, its affiliates and its and their respective Representatives, that it shall not, without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, disclose to any Person the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to you, that discussions or negotiations have taken place or are taking place concerning a possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 1 removes the phrase 'have been informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information, and have been advised that such information is to be kept confidential' and 'shall not be deemed to be your Representatives unless (but solely to the extent that) you furnish Confidential Information to them' this creates an in-text contradiction, because it later states, 'You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives'. Without a clear definition of who constitutes a 'Representative' and if they were informed of their obligations, enforcing liability for their breaches becomes uncertain, creating ambiguity in who is bound by the confidentiality terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. You hereby agree that the Confidential Information will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating and implementing the Transaction and that such information will be kept confidential by you; provided that any such information may be disclosed to your officers, directors, employees, affiliates, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, consultants, other agents or representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating and implementing the Transaction. You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives. You and the Company also each agree, on behalf of itself, its affiliates and its and their respective Representatives, that it shall not, without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, disclose to any Person the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to you, that discussions or negotiations have taken place or are taking place concerning a possible Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1 removes the requirement that 'Representatives' be informed of the confidential nature of the information and the condition that they are only considered Representatives if Confidential Information is furnished to them. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, states 'You agree you will be liable for any breaches of the terms of this Agreement by such Representatives.' By removing the definition of 'Representatives', and by removing the clause that they have been informed of their obligations, it creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding who the 'Representatives' are and what their obligations are. This makes it difficult to enforce the liability clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it relates to enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: 2. The term “Confidential Information” does not include any information which (a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to or known by the public (other than as a result of its disclosure by you or your Representatives in breach of this Agreement), (b) was available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) becomes available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a Person who is not known by you or your Representatives (as applicable) to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company, or is not otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to you or your Representatives (as applicable), or (d) is or was independently developed by you or your Representatives without reference to, incorporation of, or other use of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. The term “Confidential Information” does not include any information which (a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to or known by the public, (b) was available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) becomes available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a Person who is not known by you to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company, or is not otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to you, or (d) is or was independently developed by you or your Representatives without reference to, incorporation of, or other use of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 2 removes the phrase '(other than as a result of its disclosure by you or your Representatives in breach of this Agreement)' in (a), and '(as applicable)' from (c). These omissions weaken the restrictions on what is excluded from being confidential and create an in-text contradiction. Because later on, it states that the term does not include 'information which is generally available to or known by the public' but at the same time removes that information that was made available because your representatives disclose the information. By removing the specific exception, it implies that even if representatives improperly disclosed the information, it is no longer considered confidential, which makes contradictory the purpose of the agreement, which is for keep the information confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The term “Confidential Information” does not include any information which (a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter is generally available to or known by the public, (b) was available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis prior to disclosure by the Company, (c) becomes available to you or your Representatives on a non-confidential basis from a Person who is not known by you to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Company, or is not otherwise prohibited from transmitting the information to you, or (d) is or was independently developed by you or your Representatives without reference to, incorporation of, or other use of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 2, specifically the removal of '(other than as a result of its disclosure by you or your Representatives in breach of this Agreement)' in (a), creates a contradiction. The original text explicitly excludes information disclosed in breach of the agreement from the 'publicly available' exception to confidentiality. By removing this exclusion, the modified text implies that even information made public due to a breach by the recipient or their representatives is no longer considered confidential. This directly contradicts the core purpose of a confidentiality agreement, as it negates the protection against unauthorized disclosure, which is a strong contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this highlights that even though the same text is in both location, removing that portion does create a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information and you and the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of our discussions or by any breach of this Agreement by you, the Company or the Representatives of you and the Company. Without prejudice to other rights and remedies otherwise available, you and the Company therefore agree that the other party hereto shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction or specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party hereto or the Representatives of the other party hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information and you and the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of our discussions or by any breach of this Agreement by you or the Company. Without prejudice to other rights and remedies otherwise available, you and the Company therefore agree that the other party hereto shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction or specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 3 removes the phrases 'or the Representatives of you and the Company' in both the first and second sentences. By removing the reference to Representatives being a cause of damage and a basis for equitable relief, it weakens the scope of protection. This omission creates an in-text contradiction, as it contradicts Section 1, which states the firm is liable for breaches by representatives, but this section no longer offers a clear remedy if representatives breach the agreement. If the representatives are not part of this section, it leaves ambiguity of what would happen.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Given the nature of the Confidential Information and our current discussions, the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information and you and the Company may be irreparably damaged by any unauthorized disclosure of our discussions or by any breach of this Agreement by you or the Company. Without prejudice to other rights and remedies otherwise available, you and the Company therefore agree that the other party hereto shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including an injunction or specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party hereto.
perturbation.justification: The modified Section 3 removes 'or the Representatives of you and the Company' from the scope of the agreement's breach provision and equitable relief. This omission directly contradicts the initial intent and protection afforded, creating a contradiction because representatives were originally explicitly included.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The Company may elect at any time to terminate further access by you to the Confidential Information. Following any written request by the Company, you agree, at the Company’s election, to either promptly redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession or your Representatives’ possession, and you and your Representatives will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you or your Representatives based on the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, and you shall delete, to the extent reasonably practicable, all Confidential Information from any computer or other electronic device. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such destruction or deletion shall be 2 required where prohibited by law, regulation, regulatory process or proceeding, or stock exchange regulation, or where the Confidential Information is part of the record of proceedings of your Board of Directors; provided , that any such Confidential Information so retained shall continue to be held confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The Company may elect at any time to terminate further access by you to the Confidential Information. Following any written request by the Company, you agree, at the Company’s election, to either promptly redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession, and you will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you based on the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, and you shall delete, to the extent reasonably practicable, all Confidential Information from any computer or other electronic device. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such destruction or deletion shall be 2 required where prohibited by law, regulation, regulatory process or proceeding, or stock exchange regulation, or where the Confidential Information is part of the record of proceedings of your Board of Directors; provided , that any such Confidential Information so retained shall continue to be held confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 5 removes the phrases 'or your Representatives’ possession' and 'or your Representatives' throughout the section. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 1. Section 1 states the client is responsible for their Representatives, this sections creates a hole in how that information is handled. Meaning, a representative can keep the Confidential Information, without being liable to the contract, and that would be a breach of Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. The Company may elect at any time to terminate further access by you to the Confidential Information. Following any written request by the Company, you agree, at the Company’s election, to either promptly redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession, and you will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you based on the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, and you shall delete, to the extent reasonably practicable, all Confidential Information from any computer or other electronic device. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such destruction or deletion shall be 2 required where prohibited by law, regulation, regulatory process or proceeding, or stock exchange regulation, or where the Confidential Information is part of the record of proceedings of your Board of Directors; provided , that any such Confidential Information so retained shall continue to be held confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text held the client responsible for their representatives' actions regarding confidential information. The changed text removes the phrase 'or your Representatives’ possession' and 'or your Representatives' throughout the section, specifically Section 5. The Perturbation Explanation states that Section 1 holds the client responsible for their representatives, and by removing the representatives from Section 5, the representative can keep the Confidential Information, without being liable to the contract, and that would be a breach of Section 1. Since the changed section creates a loophole where representatives are no longer bound by the same rules regarding confidential information, it directly contradicts the principle of responsibility outlined in Section 1, creating a strong in-text contradiction as explained.

perturbation.original_text: 9, Upon the terms and subject to the conditions of a definitive agreement, if any, with respect to the Transaction and without prejudice thereto, each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that none of the other party or any of such other party’s respective Representatives and none of the respective officers, directors, partners, shareholders, members, employees, agents or persons in control of such other party’s Representatives makes any express or implied representation or warranty to such party, such party’s Representatives or to any other person as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party, and each party hereto hereby further acknowledges and agrees that none of such persons shall have any liability to the other party or any of such other party’s Representatives relating to or arising from or in connection with such other party’s use or the use by such other party’s Representatives of any Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party to such other party or its respective Representatives or for any inaccuracies contained therein or any omissions therefrom. Each party hereto also acknowledges and agrees that it is not entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information furnished to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of the other party and that such party shall be entitled to rely solely on such representations and warranties as may be made to it and contained expressly in any definitive agreement with respect to the Transaction, to the extent in accordance with the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in any such definitive agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9, Upon the terms and subject to the conditions of a definitive agreement, if any, with respect to the Transaction and without prejudice thereto, each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that none of the other party and none of the respective officers, directors, partners, shareholders, members, employees, agents or persons in control of such other party makes any express or implied representation or warranty to such party or to any other person as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party, and each party hereto hereby further acknowledges and agrees that none of such persons shall have any liability to the other party relating to or arising from or in connection with such other party’s use of any Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party to such other party or for any inaccuracies contained therein or any omissions therefrom. Each party hereto also acknowledges and agrees that it is not entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information furnished to it by or on behalf of the other party and that such party shall be entitled to rely solely on such representations and warranties as may be made to it and contained expressly in any definitive agreement with respect to the Transaction, to the extent in accordance with the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in any such definitive agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 9 removes any reference to 'Representatives' or 'other party’s respective Representatives'. By removing the protections related to any of the companies representatives, the document no longer supports that these people, who are deemed to be responsible for handling the company's confidential information, are now liable to any damages. This creates an in-text contradiction, because Section 1 dictates that these members are supposed to keep all information confidential, but also this section provides them with some type of protection that they cannot be held liable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9, Upon the terms and subject to the conditions of a definitive agreement, if any, with respect to the Transaction and without prejudice thereto, each party hereto acknowledges and agrees that none of the other party and none of the respective officers, directors, partners, shareholders, members, employees, agents or persons in control of such other party makes any express or implied representation or warranty to such party or to any other person as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party, and each party hereto hereby further acknowledges and agrees that none of such persons shall have any liability to the other party relating to or arising from or in connection with such other party’s use of any Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party to such other party or for any inaccuracies contained therein or any omissions therefrom. Each party hereto also acknowledges and agrees that it is not entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information furnished to it by or on behalf of the other party and that such party shall be entitled to rely solely on such representations and warranties as may be made to it and contained expressly in any definitive agreement with respect to the Transaction, to the extent in accordance with the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in any such definitive agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly protected the 'Representatives' of each party from liability regarding the confidential information. The changed text removes the phrase "such party’s Representatives or to any other person as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party, and each party hereto hereby further acknowledges and agrees that none of such persons shall have any liability to the other party or any of such other party’s Representatives relating to or arising from or in connection with such other party’s use or the use by such other party’s Representatives of any Confidential Information furnished by or on behalf of such party to such other party or its respective Representatives or for any inaccuracies contained therein or any omissions therefrom". This omission contradicts the initial intent by removing the Representatives' protection from liability, creating an inconsistency as these individuals are entrusted with this information but are no longer shielded. The Contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text, demonstrating that the omission of key text is creating a contradiction regarding who is liable.
